tools.name: verify
tools.description: Verify a claim deterministically — returns a verdict (HOLDS / BROKEN / INCOMPLETE), the worked trail, AND a sealed receipt {content_hash, cite_url} you can re-fetch and re-verify (seal_fetch). Two forms: (a) MATH — {mode, params}; (b) ANY DOMAIN — pass `steps`, a list of {id, domain, spec} where spe

tools.name: audit
tools.description: Audit a whole text: deterministic extractors find every checkable quantitative claim (sums, percentages, hourly/annual pay, compound interest, rule-of-72, elapsed years, day-of-week, leap years, nutrition labels), the engine verifies the lot, and ONE sealed coverage report returns — per-claim source

tools.name: search
tools.description: Ranked search over the keeping (the kept library).

tools.name: seal_fetch
tools.description: Fetch a sealed verification record (the receipt) by its content hash.

tools.name: redact
tools.description: Strip PII (emails, SSNs, credit cards, IPs, URLs) from text to stable placeholders before you pass it onward; the mapping is returned so YOU reveal replies locally. For true privacy run this on a LOCAL/sovereign engine (the text never leaves your machine) or use the client libraries — the strip belo

tools.name: card_get
tools.description: Fetch one card (the full record) from the keeping by id.

tools.name: cards_browse
tools.description: Browse the keeping — paginated, optional shelf filter. Returns card briefs.

tools.name: cards_stats
tools.description: Counts over the keeping — total, by shelf, by surface.

tools.name: daily_card
tools.description: The deterministic card of the day from the keeping (same all day).

tools.name: grid_axis
tools.description: The map: a read-only view of one axis (its scaffold members, depth, neighbors, umbrella children). Omit `axis` for an overview of all axes.

tools.name: grid_dimension
tools.description: The axes that sit on a given scaffold member (dimension).

tools.name: card_connections
tools.description: Cards related to one card — its explicit links + same-shelf siblings.

tools.name: locate
tools.description: Find the card for a query — by exact id, then title, else ranked search.

tools.name: library_health
tools.description: Corpus health — is the keeping loaded and sound (totals, shelves, surfaces).

tools.name: pronounce
tools.description: A synthesized pronunciation guide (respelling + approximate IPA) for a transliteration or word — honestly labeled, not a native speaker.

tools.name: steward_budget
tools.description: Steward — a household budget (income, expenses -> net, savings rate, by category). Shows and plans; NEVER moves money.

tools.name: steward_cost_destroyed
tools.description: Steward — cost destroyed: money you did NOT spend (was -> now), kept in your currency.

tools.name: coach_subjects
tools.description: Coach — the subjects a learner can study (read / mcguffey / aesop / founding / pilgrims / es / …), each with its unit count. 'read' is the door in.

tools.name: coach_overview
tools.description: Coach (K-3 tutor) — one subject's whole path: unit count, tracks, ordered unit briefs. ?subject= selects the path (default 'read'). Verbatim; never generated.

tools.name: coach_unit
tools.description: Coach — one unit, VERBATIM as authored (rule, examples, decodable sentence, checks).

tools.name: coach_next
tools.description: Coach — the next lesson in a subject, deterministically. Omit `after` for the first unit; pass a unit id for the one that follows it. `subject` selects the path.

tools.name: coach_recommend
tools.description: Coach — adaptive 'what's next' in a subject: given completed unit ids, the next lesson whose prerequisites are met (grows with the student). Found, never generated.

tools.name: coach_mastery
tools.description: Coach — seal an HONEST INTEGER count of completed units (a receipt for progress, never a grade on the child). Returns a re-checkable seal.

tools.name: coach_guidance
tools.description: Coach — what it does and the boundary it will not cross (never grades a child).

tools.name: identity_create
tools.description: Mint a SOVEREIGN, PORTABLE identity the person OWNS — {id, public_key, private_key, signing_available}. The private_key is returned ONCE and is NEVER stored server-side; hand it to the user and forget it.

tools.name: identity_verify
tools.description: Verify a signature over a message against a public key (never raises; True/False).

tools.name: identity_fingerprint
tools.description: Derive the stable public fingerprint id from a public key (deterministic).

tools.name: badges_issue
tools.description: Issue a badge over already-sealed checks — a re-checkable receipt that points at N seals that STILL STAND. States EXACTLY N; NEVER a competency claim.

tools.name: badges_verify
tools.description: Re-check a badge from the store — re-verifies every seal it references and returns the count that still stands (N recomputed, not trusted).

tools.name: self_attest
tools.description: Record a person's OWN words about their study — a DISTINCTLY TYPED record that can NEVER count as a sealed check or satisfy an auto-graded requirement.

tools.name: study_create
tools.description: Create/extend a shared study (superposition stack) — each entry mints ONE card that lives once and is referenced by key; no duplication.

tools.name: study_export
tools.description: Export a study as a self-contained, portable bundle (optionally signed).

tools.name: study_import
tools.description: Import an exported study bundle — re-materializes its cards (each lives once).

tools.name: groups_list
tools.description: Discover pseudonymous shared-study groups by TOPIC (not by person). Optional q filters over topic/title/description. Members are handles only — no PII.

tools.name: group_get
tools.description: A study group: topic, member handles (no ids/PII), and the shared-study cards.

tools.name: group_create
tools.description: Open a study group around a topic (pseudonymous; a handle, no personal info). For grown believers — the children's coach is a separate, never-joined surface.

tools.name: group_join
tools.description: Join a study group (consent-based, pseudonymous; idempotent).

tools.name: group_contribute
tools.description: Add a verse/note/question to a group's shared study — attributed to your handle, optionally signed. Verbatim; a member's own words, not engine-verified.

findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "verify" is 617 chars, far longer than this server's median (102). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

evidence.snippet: Verify a claim deterministically — returns a verdict (HOLDS / BROKEN / INCOMPLETE), the worked trail, AND a sealed receipt {content_hash, cite_url} you can re-f…

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.
